weather: Cloudy and cool in Edmonds today. Current 48°F, feels like 44°F with 83 % humidity and light south wind 8 mph. Overcast skies dominate, with only a 15–20 % chance of light rain—mainly before late afternoon. Daytime highs struggle to the low-50s; evening stays cloudy and damp, lows near 47°F. A typical late-spring marine layer: no heavy rain, just gray and brisk—jacket weather for any outings with Piper.

local_news.findings: A 58-year-old woman died in her cell at Snohomish County Jail on Wednesday morning. The woman was found unresponsive during routine checks at 7:25 a.m. in the jail's medical housing unit. Staff attempted lifesaving measures but were unsuccessful. She had been booked into jail on April 20 for suspicion of loitering, possession of a controlled substance, and a Stay Out of Drug Areas violation. An unexpected fatality review and separate death investigation are being conducted.

The City of Edmonds hosted an open house Thursday to share the 60% design plan for Stage 3 of the Highway 99 Revitalization Project. The public is being asked to provide input on the design plans as the project moves forward. This represents continued progress on the major infrastructure improvement initiative for Highway 99 in Edmonds.

Edmonds School District is offering extensive summer learning opportunities for 2026, including programs for various grade levels and interests. Some programs are open to all students regardless of district enrollment. Additionally, Edmonds-Woodway High School's Jazz Program earned top honors at the 59th annual Lionel Hampton Jazz Festival in Moscow, Idaho, bringing home several top prizes.

openings.summary: Multiple continuing-contract positions for 2026-27; requires Spanish proficiency and preference given to ELL/ESOL/Bilingual endorsements.
Full-time pool for 2026-27; district will draw from this list as specific openings arise.
General postings open April–June and again in August; applications reviewed for building-specific openings district-wide.
Certificated openings posted year-round; use SchoolSpring to apply to specific secondary positions.
Ongoing certificated postings; check SchoolSpring for current secondary vacancies and apply online.
Grades 9-12 English openings; district emphasizes culturally responsive instruction and data-driven teaching.
Large district with frequent postings on Indeed and LinkedIn; typical salary range $78k–$143k.
Dozens of openings city-wide; pay spans $53k–$228k depending on experience and credentials.

career.notes: All districts accept applications through their own portals or via SchoolSpring/Indeed. Edmonds and Snohomish have already posted 2026-27 pools; Lake Washington and Northshore will refresh postings in late spring. Ensure WA state teaching certificate and endorsements are current before applying.

global_news.findings: Iran has offered to reopen the Strait of Hormuz if the U.S. lifts its blockade and the war ends, according to two regional officials. The proposal, delivered via intermediaries, would see Iran end its chokehold on the vital waterway in exchange for the U.S. ending its naval blockade and ceasing military operations. The offer comes as both nations dispatched negotiators to Pakistan for new talks after previous delays.

Russian drones attacked Ukraine's southern port city of Odesa, injuring at least 11 people including two children, and damaging homes and critical infrastructure. The attack affected three districts, hitting residential buildings, vehicles, and civilian facilities including a hotel and warehouses. This comes as Russia also claimed Ukraine conducted a drone attack on the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, killing one worker.

A Russian drone attack on Ukraine's southern city of Odesa wounded more than a dozen people, including two children, and damaged residential buildings. The overnight attack represents the latest escalation in Russia's ongoing campaign against Ukrainian civilian infrastructure.

Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old from California, has been identified as the suspect in the White House Correspondents' Dinner shooting incident. The suspect was stopped by law enforcement before reaching the dinner where President Trump and 2,600 guests had gathered. A Secret Service officer was injured during the incident, and the suspect is due to appear in federal court Monday.

triadic_ontology.findings: The Routledge volume *Triadic Ontology and Quantum Perichoresis: A Relational Metaphysics for the 21st Century* by Sarah Coakley (2026) extends her earlier work by integrating quantum field-theoretic notions of entanglement with the patristic concept of perichoresis. Coakley argues that non-linear triadic dynamics—where relations are ontologically prior to relata—offer a resolution to the measurement problem in quantum mechanics, proposing that observer, observed, and relational field form an irreducible triad whose symmetry-breaking events generate actuality. In *Process Studies* 55:1, Andrew M. Davis responds with "Perichoretic Panentheism and Triadic Logic," critiquing Coakley’s model for underplaying the temporal asymmetry of quantum collapse and suggesting a trivalent logic (true, false, indeterminate) that remains open to future determination. Meanwhile, the Karl-Alber series releases *Studies on Triadic Ontology, Vol. 4: Non-Linear Dynamics and Trinitarian Semantics* (eds. Klaus [TRUNCATED]

ai_systems.findings: The 2026 landscape of autonomous research systems is defined by a decisive shift from monolithic agents to disciplined multi-agent orchestration. Google’s PaperOrchestra now autonomously converts raw lab notes into submission-ready LaTeX manuscripts in ~40 minutes, using five specialized agents that outperform single-agent baselines by 39–86 % on overall quality and 88–99 % on literature-review depth. Sakana AI’s newly released Fugu API commercializes this approach, dynamically routing tasks among frontier models to hit SOTA on SWE-Pro and GPQA-D while exposing simple OpenAI-compatible endpoints. On the research front, the open-source Mimosa framework (arXiv 2603.28986) introduces an “evolving workflow” layer that rewrites its own agent topologies based on experimental feedback, pushing success rates on ScienceAgentBench to 43.1 %. LangChain’s Deep Agents stack adds production-grade persistence and sub-agent delegation, enabling multi-hour research pipelines that survive crashes via Mo [TRUNCATED]

uap.findings: Congressional momentum on UAP transparency accelerated this week. The House Oversight Committee convened a closed-door classified briefing on 24 April, attended by members of the newly-formed UAP Transparency Caucus led by Rep. Moskowitz. While no official readout was released, sources tell The Aviationist that lawmakers pressed DoD officials on the slow pace of document release and the security protocols surrounding alleged non-human craft recovery programs. Separately, the Pentagon’s AARO office quietly published a 21 April addendum to its 2025 Historical Report, acknowledging for the first time that 14 incidents between 1947-1969 remain “unresolved and potentially attributable to non-human technology.” The 11-page addendum, posted without press notice, cites newly declassified CIA telemetry logs and radar data from decommissioned NORAD sites. NASA’s independent UAP study team issued its own interim update on 26 April, recommending that Congress fund a dedicated $50 million annual ob [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.text:
![Site Logo](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2026/02/HeraldNet2.png)
![](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/themes/2024-cmg/media/img/Search_icon-black.png)

## Woman, 58, died in her cell at Snohomish County Jail

Published 4:18 pm Wednesday, April 22, 2026

**By [Jenna Millikan](https://www.heraldnet.com/author/jenna-millikan/)**

![The Snohomish County Jail is pictured on Thursday, Oct. 26, 2023, in Everett, Washington. (Ryan Berry / The Herald)](https://www.heraldnet.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/8/2026/04/42139406_web1_L1-SnoCoJail-EDH-231026-FS.jpg?w=1024)

The Snohomish County Jail is pictured on Thursday, Oct. 26, 2023, in Everett, Washington. (Ryan Berry / The Herald)

EVERETT — An inmate died inside her cell Wednesday morning at Snohomish County Jail.

Around 7:25 a.m. Wednesday, a corrections deputy found a 58-year-old woman unresponsive inside her cell at the Snohomish County Jail during routine checks, according to a Snohomish County Sheriff’s Office press release. She was housed in the jail’s medical housing unit.

Corrections staff declared a medical emergency and began lifesaving measures, but resuscitation attempts were unsuccessful, the relea [TRUNCATED]

enriched_articles.title: Russia attacks Odesa, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant | Russia-Ukraine war News | Al Jazeera
enriched_articles.text:
# Russia attacks Odesa, claims Ukraine hit Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant

*A Ukrainian attack on the captured Zaporizhzhia nuclear plant kills a worker, according to the site’s Russia-installed authorities.*

Save

Share

![A rescue worker walks inside apartments destroyed by a Russian strike in Odesa, Ukraine, Monday, April 27, 2026. (AP Photo/Michael Shtekel)](/wp-content/uploads/2026/04/ap_69ef215798181-1777279319.jpg?resize=770%2C513&quality=80)
![](/wp-content/uploads/2024/05/2017-06-08T131939Z_1189275883_RC11B0FE7F20_RTRMADP_3_GULF-QATAR-JAZEERA-1714943623.jpg?resize=96%2C96&quality=80)

Ukrainian officials say Russian drones have again attacked the southern port city of Odesa, injuring at least 11 people, including two children, and damaging homes and important infrastructure.

Odesa Governor Oleh Kiper said the attack affected three districts, hitting residential buildings, vehicles and civilian facilities, including a hotel, warehouses and funicular railway. Windows shattered in many buildings and the port area sustained damage.

## Recommended Stories
